signals.question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 15?
evidence: Iran explicitly rejected US 15-point ceasefire plan on March 25, calling it 'maximalist, unreasonable' — Al Jazeera https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/25/iran-calls-us-proposal-to-end-war-maximalist-unreasonable

Iran FM Abbas Araghchi told state TV 'no peace talks with the US have taken place' and Iran has 'no plans for negotiations' — NBC News https://www.nbcnews.com/world/middle-east/live-blog/live-updates-iran-war-talks-trump-peace-plan-troops-hormuz-rcna265051

Both sides hardening positions as of March 27; Pentagon considering sending 10,000 additional troops — PBS https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/iran-and-the-u-s-harden-their-positions-as-tehran-tightens-its-grip-on-the-strait-of-hormuz

Suspected insider Polymarket accounts bet $2M on US forces entering Iran by April 30 (escalation, not de-escalation) — Times of Israel https://www.timesofisrael.com/polymarket-bets-on-us-iran-ceasefire-appear-to-suggest-insider-trading/

Resolution requires 'clear public confirmation from both governments' — extremely high bar when no direct talks exist; only indirect messages via Pakistan mediator — OPB https://www.opb.org/article/2026/03/26/us-iran-indirect-talks-confirmed-by-pakistan-as-war-rages/
signals.reasoning: Iran has publicly rejected the US ceasefire plan, denied any negotiations are occurring, and hardened its 5-point demand (including war reparations and Hormuz sovereignty). With no direct talks, active military escalation, and only 19 days until close, 27.5% dramatically overestimates the probability of a formal ceasefire requiring public confirmation from both governments.

signals.question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 30?
evidence: Iran rejected US 15-point ceasefire plan March 25; Iran's 5 counterdemands include reparations and Strait of Hormuz sovereignty — NPR https://www.npr.org/2026/03/25/nx-s1-5760675/iran-war-military-deployment
Iran FM: 'no peace talks with US have taken place'; only indirect messages through mediators — NBC News, OPB

Military escalation continuing: IDF struck Iranian missile facility in Yazd; Pentagon considering 10,000 additional ground troops — Al Jazeera https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/26/us-israel-war-on-iran-whats-happening-on-day-27-of-attacks
Suspected insider accounts betting on escalation (US ground forces in Iran by April 30), not de-escalation — Newsweek https://www.newsweek.com/iran-war-bets-fuel-insider-trading-fears-11736026
Historical base rate: wars of this scale (US/Israel vs regional power) rarely produce formal ceasefires within 2 months of initiation, especially with fundamental disagreements on terms
signals.reasoning: The fundamental diplomatic impasse — Iran refusing talks, demanding reparations and Hormuz sovereignty, while the US escalates militarily — makes a formal ceasefire requiring public confirmation from both sides within 34 days highly unlikely. At 40.5%, the market significantly overprices YES given zero direct negotiations and active military escalation.

signals.question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 7?
evidence: Iran rejected US ceasefire plan 2 days ago; FM denies any talks are occurring — Al Jazeera, NBC News
Trump extended energy strike pause deadline to April 6 — but Iran has not engaged in response — NPR https://www.npr.org/2026/03/26/nx-s1-5761882/iran-war-peace-conditions
Only 11 days until close; no direct talks, no framework for agreement; resolution requires mutual public confirmation from both governments
signals.reasoning: With Iran publicly refusing talks, active military escalation, and only 11 days remaining, a formal ceasefire requiring public confirmation from both governments by April 7 is near-impossible. Trump's energy pause deadline expires April 6 — if anything, escalation resumes before this market closes.
